scenes.background: radial-gradient(ellipse 70% 50% at 12% 14%, rgba(244, 63, 94, 0.22) 0%, transparent 55%), radial-gradient(ellipse 60% 50% at 88% 92%, rgba(139, 92, 246, 0.22) 0%, transparent 55%), radial-gradient(circle at 50% 50%, #0b0d18 0%, #050610 100%)

elements.content:
4 pieces run the feed

A social app backbone is basically: API layer, server, database, and storage — all working behind a cloud boundary.

scenes.background: radial-gradient(ellipse 70% 50% at 12% 14%, rgba(244, 63, 94, 0.22) 0%, transparent 55%), radial-gradient(ellipse 60% 50% at 88% 92%, rgba(139, 92, 246, 0.22) 0%, transparent 55%), radial-gradient(circle at 50% 50%, #0b0d18 0%, #050610 100%)

elements.content:
The API layer is the front door

Every app request enters here first. It checks the request, then sends it to the backend that can handle it.

- Receives mobile/web traffic
- Validates and routes requests
- Keeps internal services behind one entry point

scenes.background: radial-gradient(ellipse 70% 50% at 12% 14%, rgba(244, 63, 94, 0.22) 0%, transparent 55%), radial-gradient(ellipse 60% 50% at 88% 92%, rgba(139, 92, 246, 0.22) 0%, transparent 55%), radial-gradient(circle at 50% 50%, #0b0d18 0%, #050610 100%)

elements.content:
The server runs the logic

The server decides what should happen: load a feed, create a post, check permissions, or connect data to media.

- Business rules live here
- Talks to database for records
- Talks to storage for files

scenes.background: radial-gradient(ellipse 70% 50% at 12% 14%, rgba(244, 63, 94, 0.22) 0%, transparent 55%), radial-gradient(ellipse 60% 50% at 88% 92%, rgba(139, 92, 246, 0.22) 0%, transparent 55%), radial-gradient(circle at 50% 50%, #0b0d18 0%, #050610 100%)

elements.content:
Read path: fetch the feed

A read request goes in, data comes back out. The database is where posts, likes, follows, and comments are loaded from.

- Client asks for feed
- Server reads records
- Response returns to the app

scenes.background: radial-gradient(ellipse 70% 50% at 12% 14%, rgba(244, 63, 94, 0.22) 0%, transparent 55%), radial-gradient(ellipse 60% 50% at 88% 92%, rgba(139, 92, 246, 0.22) 0%, transparent 55%), radial-gradient(circle at 50% 50%, #0b0d18 0%, #050610 100%)

elements.content:
Write path: publish a post

For a photo post, the file and the post record are different things. Media goes to storage; metadata goes to the database.

- Upload image or video
- Save post text and media reference
- Return publish confirmation

architecture-beta
    group api(cloud)[Cloud API]

    service client(internet)[Client]
    service gateway(internet)[Gateway] in api
    service server(server)[Server] in api
    service media(disk)[Media Storage] in api
    service db(database)[Database] in api

    client:R -- L:gateway
    gateway:R -- L:server
    server:R -- L:media
    server:B -- T:db

scenes.background: radial-gradient(ellipse 70% 50% at 12% 14%, rgba(244, 63, 94, 0.22) 0%, transparent 55%), radial-gradient(ellipse 60% 50% at 88% 92%, rgba(139, 92, 246, 0.22) 0%, transparent 55%), radial-gradient(circle at 50% 50%, #0b0d18 0%, #050610 100%)

elements.content:
“Boundaries matter.”

The API layer is public-facing. The database and storage stay behind it, protected by backend logic.

scenes.background: radial-gradient(ellipse 70% 50% at 12% 14%, rgba(244, 63, 94, 0.22) 0%, transparent 55%), radial-gradient(ellipse 60% 50% at 88% 92%, rgba(139, 92, 246, 0.22) 0%, transparent 55%), radial-gradient(circle at 50% 50%, #0b0d18 0%, #050610 100%)

elements.content:
Roles recap

If you remember one thing: every component has one clean job, and that keeps the system easier to scale and debug.

- API layer handles requests
- Server runs business logic
- Database stores app data
- Storage keeps media files

scenes.background: radial-gradient(ellipse 70% 50% at 12% 14%, rgba(244, 63, 94, 0.22) 0%, transparent 55%), radial-gradient(ellipse 60% 50% at 88% 92%, rgba(139, 92, 246, 0.22) 0%, transparent 55%), radial-gradient(circle at 50% 50%, #0b0d18 0%, #050610 100%)

elements.content:
architecture-beta
    group api(cloud)[Cloud API]

    service client(internet)[Client App]
    service gateway(internet)[API Layer] in api
    service server(server)[Server] in api
    service db(database)[Database] in api
    service media(disk)[Storage] in api

    client:R -- L:gateway
    gateway:R -- L:server
    server:B -- T:db
    server:R -- L:media

The backbone in 90 seconds

Your device talks to the cloud, the API routes the request, the server does the work, and the data stores keep everything persistent.

- Device → API layer
- API layer → server
- Server → database and storage
- Response → back to the app
